text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text: As I pass through the central plaza...

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Oh-ho? Is that you, Cafe? I note your<n>gaze is
directed quite far off into the<n>distance...

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
What is it that you're looking at?<n>What presence
does that brain of<n>yours detect?

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: ...Nothing special. Just the usual.

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text:
My friend went on a run... so I was<n>just watching
them.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
AHAHAHA! That "friend" of yours<n>again, eh? Ah,
you truly are a<n>fascinating case!

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text: You gaze into nothingness, and yetyou insist that someone's there!

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Whether you speak falsehood or<n>truth, I must
know what's inside of<n>that head of yours! Say
there, Cafe,<n>would you allow me to take a little
peek at your brain?!

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: ...I'll have to refuse.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Whaaat? Now, now! Don't be like<n>that! This is the
perfect opportunity to<n>uncover the unknowns of
Umakind!

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text: ...Achoo!

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: Sigh...

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Goodness, you're such a wet blanket<n>today... and
every other day.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
...Her claims seem too concrete to be<n>lies, and yet,
there's no hard<n>evidence to prove their veracity.
Low<n>probability of this being a defense
mechanism... No abnormalities with<n>her nervous
system. I have to<n>examine her brain...

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: ...You're still here?

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text: Oh ho, Cafe?! What brings you back?

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text: Could it be that you're burning withcuriousity? Yes! It's only natural thatyou'd become interested!

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: It's... going to rain.

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text:
The air has been getting colder for a<n>while now...
I'm sure you feel cold<n>too...

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text:
I brought some hot coffee... Enough<n>for... two
people.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
...Hmm. And that's why you<n>returned? Or rather,
that's what you<n>left for in the first place?

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Haha! Then you should have said<n>something!
Although I'd like to add<n>that perhaps an umbrella
would have<n>been more approp—

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: I'll drink them both if you don't want<n>one.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text: No, don't be silly! I'd love one!

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Now then, let's go before the rain<n>starts in
earnest... Hmm.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Ah, yes! I've actually developed a<n>solution that
transforms any coffee<n>into a saccharine delight
with just a<n>single drop! Please participate in my
exp—

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: No thank you.

text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
And with that, the two of them return<n>to the
academy.
